design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through theme park stages by defeating waves of enemies (security guards, mascots, pirates) using character-specific special attacks
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Attrition-based combat where enemy durability creates prolonged engagement windows
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playable chaos at a theme park; control of LaShawn or Kahlil causing mayhem through Fun World (Disneyland-inspired setting)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Heavily durability-gated combat design that prioritizes enemy resilience over player execution skill, creating grinding-style beat 'em up gameplay

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Limited; difficulty stems from enemy damage thresholds rather than player-selectable options. Character selection (LaShawn vs Kahlil) provides differentiated special attack strategies

progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage progression through theme park locations: haunted house, pirate ship, the pits, and other areas
replayability_hooks: Two playable characters with unique special attacks
Multiple stage environments with thematic variety

world_building_techniques: Licensed film property (1992 Bebe's Kids film) providing narrative context
Thematic stage environments (Fun World theme park setting)

danger_signals: Enemy waves scaling in number and resilience across stages
Themed adversaries suggesting systematic opposition from park management
environmental_storytelling: Fun World setting (Disneyland analog) as corporate/entertainment space under player disruption
Haunted house, pirate ship locations establishing thematic stage progression
NPC mascots as corrupted representations of entertainment industry
